name: Chat Multi-Channel
description: Full-featured messaging with public/private groups, DMs, member management, and channel settings
details: Extends chat-messaging with multi-channel navigation. Sidebar shows groups (public/private) and direct messages. Users can create groups, browse public groups, start DMs, manage members, and leave groups. On mobile, the sidebar and chat area swap with a push/pop transition.
instructions: Install chat-messaging first (provides schemas, MessageList, ChatHeader, ThreadPanel, etc.)
Add to App.tsx: import ChatMultiPage from './pages/ChatMultiPage'
Replace the ChatPage route with: <ProtectedRoute allowedRoles={[ROLES.MEMBER, ROLES.ADMIN]}><ChatMultiPage /></ProtectedRoute>
Parent route container needs a definite height (h-full or flex-1) — ChatMultiPage uses h-full
The chat-messaging ChatPage is still available at ./pages/ChatPage for single-channel use if preferred
patterns: useMultiChannel() → { activeChannelId, selectChannel, groups, dms, pendingInvitations, hasUnread, getDMDisplayName }
Channel types: 'public' (join via Browse), 'private' (invitation-only), 'dm' (auto-join on open)
Private group invitations: AddMemberModal creates pending → InvitationList accept/decline
Reuses chat-messaging components (MessageList, ThreadPanel, etc.) for all messaging functionality
